interactive_prompts.name: cli_feedback_rating
interactive_prompts.regex: How's the CLI experience so far\?|Help us improve:
interactive_prompts.note: Quick feedback rating prompt from the CLI wrapper; '0' skips/cancels

interactive_prompts.name: trust_prompt
interactive_prompts.regex: Do you trust the contents of this project|Antigravity CLI requires permission
interactive_prompts.note: agy asks to trust dir on first launch; default selection is 'Yes, I trust this folder'. `once`: one-time boot dialog — must not re-fire after a single response (its text lingers in the scrollback capture during the artifact-wait loop).

interactive_prompts.name: quota_exhausted
interactive_prompts.note: Distinguishable from transient rate-limit; usually requires plan upgrade

interactive_prompts.name: permission_prompt
interactive_prompts.note: Belt-and-suspenders alongside --dangerously-skip-permissions; harmless if bypass already accepted it

interactive_prompts.name: session_rating
interactive_prompts.note: agy end-of-session rating dialog; '4' = neutral skip

note: Interactive agy via tmux. Preserves Google AI Pro subscription billing (OAuth). REPL marker is the '? for shortcuts' footer text — unique to the idle state. model_tier is channel=flag via --model (probed live 2026-07-02, agy 1.0.15: `agy --model "Claude Opus 4.6 (Thinking)"` boots the REPL in ~2s with the model in banner+footer; `agy models` lists the selectable tokens, which are display names with spaces/parens — launchCmdLine shell-quotes every realized flag token). `-m` remains UNDEFINED ('flags provided but not defined: -m'), so the cycle-154 incident claim about -m still holds; agy 1.0.3 had no model flag at all and channel was noop 2026-05-31..2026-07-02 (docs/incidents/cycle-154-agy-tmux-m-flag-repl-boot-timeout.md). model_tier_map holds offline defaults only; the LIVE model catalog overlays real per-tier picks at LoadManifest (catalog_overlay.go). See docs/architecture/cli-capability-matrix.md.
